draft.introduction: Arjuna asks about the nature of faith in those who worship outside Vedic prescription. Krishna explains that faith itself comes in three types corresponding to the three guṇas.

translation.text: Arjuna said: Those who, setting aside the ordinances of scripture, sacrifice with faith—what is their condition? Is it sattva, rajas, or tamas? The Blessed Lord said: The faith of each person is in accordance with his nature. A person consists of his faith; as a man’s faith is, so is he.
translation.original_text: arjuna uvāca | ye śāstra-vidhim utsṛjya yajante śraddhāyānvitāḥ

translator_notes.note: The statement “as a man’s faith is, so is he” (śraddhāmayo ’yaṃ puruṣaḥ) is one of the Gita’s most psychologically penetrating claims. Faith is not a doctrine one holds but the fundamental orientation of one’s being. You are what you trust.
